  #1 [MEDIUM] Unsafe temporary file creation
  File     : /Users/Shared/Projects/d/hw/dotnet-tools/tree-sitter/HW.DotNet.Parsers.Generators.TreeSitter.Private/externals/native/benchmark.sh:225
  Rule     : SH-003
  OWASP    : A01:2025 - Broken Access Control
  CWE      : CWE-377  CVSS: 5.5
  Desc     : Creating predictable temp files enables symlink attacks (race condition).
  Code     :
                 
                 if [ -n "$ts_cli" ]; then
                     # Use tree-sitter CLI to parse sample code
                     local tmpfile=$(mktemp)
                     echo "$sample_code" > "$tmpfile"
                     
                     local start_time=$(date +%s%N 2>/dev/null || date +%s)
  Fix      : Use mktemp for temp file creation. Set restrictive umask. Use process substitution <() where possible.
  Refs     : https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/377.html

  #2 [HIGH] Command injection via eval
  File     : /Users/Shared/Projects/d/hw/dotnet-tools/tree-sitter/HW.DotNet.Parsers.Generators.TreeSitter.Private/externals/native/build-all.sh:8
  Rule     : SH-001
  OWASP    : A05:2025 - Injection
  CWE      : CWE-78  CVSS: 9.8
  Desc     : eval with user-controlled input enables arbitrary command execution.
  Code     :
             set -euo pipefail
             
             if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
                 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$(eval 'echo ${(%):-%x}')")" && pwd)"
             elif [ -n "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" ]; then
                 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
             else
  Fix      : Avoid eval. Use proper shell constructs. Quote all variables: "$variable"
  Refs     : https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/78.html

  #3 [HIGH] Missing Content Security Policy (CSP)
  File     : /Users/Shared/Projects/d/hw/dotnet-tools/tree-sitter/HW.DotNet.Parsers.Generators.TreeSitter.Private/externals/native/core/tree-sitter-master/crates/cli/src/playground.html:2
  Rule     : HTML-002
  OWASP    : A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration
  CWE      : CWE-693  CVSS: 6.1
  Desc     : Pages without CSP headers are more vulnerable to XSS attacks.
  Code     :
             <!DOCTYPE html>
             <html>
               <head>
                 <meta charset="utf-8">
                 <title>tree-sitter THE_LANGUAGE_NAME</title>
  Fix      : Add Content-Security-Policy meta tag or HTTP header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'
  Refs     : https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP
